## SSS2 Physics Scheme of Work — Term 1: Mechanics
**Curriculum Board:** NERDC | **Education Level:** SSS2 | **Duration:** 13 Weeks

### Learning Objectives
Students will:
- Analyse the relationship between force, mass, and acceleration using Newton's Laws
- Compare linear and projectile motion using graphical methods
- Distinguish between scalar and vector quantities
- Examine real-world applications of mechanical principles in Nigerian infrastructure

---

| Week | Topic | Sub-topic | Learning Objectives | Activities | Assessment |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Week 1 | Introduction to Mechanics | Scalars and vectors | Distinguish scalars from vectors; examine examples from daily life | Aminu and Ngozi sort cards: speed vs velocity, distance vs displacement | Class discussion |
| Week 2 | Linear Motion | Equations of motion | Analyse motion graphs; compare uniform and non-uniform motion | Distance-time graph of danfo bus route Lagos–Ibadan | Short quiz |
| Week 3 | Linear Motion | Velocity-time graphs | Examine acceleration from gradient; calculate area = displacement | Group plot velocity-time graphs from stopwatch data on school field | Worksheet |
| Week 4 | Newton's Laws | First Law (inertia) | Analyse why passengers lurch forward during sudden braking of a bus | Demo: wooden block on paper tablecloth; Ibrahim explains inertia | Oral Q&A |
| Week 5 | Newton's Laws | Second Law (F=ma) | Calculate force using F = ma; contrast heavy vs light objects | Trolley and pulley experiment; vary mass and compare accelerations | Lab report |
| Week 6 | Newton's Laws | Third Law | Examine action-reaction pairs; analyse rocket propulsion | Balloon rocket demonstration; Chioma identifies action-reaction pairs | Written exercise |
| Week 7 | Projectile Motion | Horizontal projection | Analyse horizontal and vertical components separately | Throw a ball horizontally from a table; measure range vs height | Practical |
| Week 8 | Projectile Motion | Oblique projection | Compare ranges at different projection angles; examine max range at 45° | Computer simulation (if available) or scale drawings | Quiz |
| Week 9 | Work, Energy, Power | Definitions and units | Distinguish work, energy, and power using Nigerian construction examples | Calculate work done lifting bags of cement; Tunde measures power output | Problem set |
| Week 10 | Work, Energy, Power | Conservation of energy | Analyse energy transformations in a pendulum and a hydro dam | Pendulum experiment; examine energy flow in Kainji Dam (diagram) | Worksheet |
| Week 11 | Machines | Simple machines | Examine mechanical advantage; contrast effort vs load in levers and pulleys | Practical station rotation: lever, pulley, inclined plane | Lab report |
| Week 12 | Revision | All Term 1 topics | Analyse past WAEC questions; distinguish common misconceptions | Group problem-solving; peer teaching sessions | Mock test |
| Week 13 | End-of-Term Assessment | All topics | Examine retention across all mechanics topics | Written examination | Term exam |
reference: NERDC SSS2 Physics Scheme of Work for Term 1 covering Mechanics — scalars/vectors, Newton's Laws, projectile motion, and energy — with Nigerian real-world examples.
subject: physics

notes: Judge score 0.63 below soft threshold 0.70; flagged for human review. Pedagogy dimension low — scheme lacks explicit differentiation strategies for mixed-ability SSS2 classes.
